text: "That's all right; we already have all your money.  But it was certainly nice of you to offer."
speaker: slink

text: "Hey!  You don't have enough money to be worth our while.  How do you expect us poor independent businessmen to make a living?"
speaker: slink

text: Slink: "Thanks, it's been a pleasure doing business with you.  You can find your way out, I'm sure.  But better hurry before my dagger starts to slip."
say: Thanks, it's been a pleasure doing business with you. You can find your way out, I'm sure. But better hurry before my dagger starts to slip.
speaker: slink

text: Slink:  "My finger is starting to get a bit twitchy, so forget about questions, just pay the man and get out alive."
say: My finger is starting to get a bit twitchy, so forget about questions, just pay the man and get out alive.
speaker: slink

text: As you start to draw your weapon, the thieves jump you.
speaker: narrator

text: Before you can begin to use any magic, the thieves decide that you're up to no good.
speaker: narrator

text: Slink says, "That coin light spell gets them every time.  See that dagger?  The next one is centered on your back.  Give Sneak there your cash and you walk out of here.  It's a simple trade, your life for your money."
say: That coin light spell gets them every time. See that dagger? The next one is centered on your back. Give Sneak there your cash and you walk out of here. It's a simple trade, your life for your money.
speaker: slink

text: Slink:  "Why, how nice to see you again.  I do hope (for your sake) you brought us some nice shiny new silver."
say: Why, how nice to see you again. I do hope (for your sake) you brought us some nice shiny new silver.
speaker: slink

text: Slink:  "Hey, Sneak, this guy's hard of hearing or something.  I told him to get a license last time he was here, and he didn't listen."
say: Hey, Sneak, this guy's hard of hearing or something. I told him to get a license last time he was here, and he didn't listen.
speaker: slink

text: "Let's teach him a little lesson about listening to his betters."
speaker: slink

text: As Slink reaches for his dagger, you draw your Thieves' Guild license. . .
speaker: narrator

text: You *almost* make it.
speaker: narrator

text: Slink:  "Hey, Sneak, this guy's hard of hearing or something.  I told him to stay out of our alley, and he didn't listen."
say: Hey, Sneak, this guy's hard of hearing or something. I told him to stay out of our alley, and he didn't listen.
speaker: slink

text: You win by a close margin.
speaker: narrator

text: Slink:  "All right, so you got your license.  How nice.  Guess we don't get to kill you right now."
say: All right, so you got your license. How nice. Guess we don't get to kill you right now.
speaker: slink

text: "But stay out of our alley, 'cause we got real short memories, like."
speaker: slink

text: Perhaps you shouldn't explore dark alleys at night unless you are looking for trouble.  Or perhaps you should look in your manual to learn how to deal with thieves.
speaker: narrator

text: This room is full of odds, ends, and stuff.  The wizard must be a real character.
speaker: narrator

text: Through the portal to the east is a large formal dining room.
speaker: narrator

text: Through the portal to the west is a small sitting room.
speaker: narrator

text: Against the east wall, you see a bookcase and a desk, above which is hung a dragon's head trophy.  These are on either side of a doorway, above which hangs a shield.
speaker: narrator

text: Leaning against the west wall is a mummy case.  Above that hangs an ancient tapestry.
speaker: narrator

text: The wall is plain.  You see nothing but the front door of the house.
speaker: narrator

text: Above the staircase hangs a portrait, flanked by decorative wall hangings.  In an alcove on the landing stands a suit of armor.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a table up against the staircase wall, and on the wall hangs an onklunk.
speaker: narrator

text: It was a moderately large and fearful dragon at one time.
speaker: narrator

text: The Ball of Light floats magically over the center of the room.
speaker: narrator

text: You admire the plumage on the peacock.
speaker: narrator

text: The poor stuffed peacock seems bedraggled, worn, and over-used.
speaker: narrator

text:
You can make out the runes imprinted on the
strange device:  "Acme Toy Co."
speaker: narrator

text: Oh my!  It's a Dunking Dragon!  You thought they were only to be found in myths and legends.
speaker: narrator

text: It is a standard roll-top desk.  There is nothing on it.
speaker: narrator

text:
The suit of armor was made for someone much taller than you.   The plaque underneath it reads:
  "Bequeathed by Colonel Gulden Dijon."
speaker: narrator

text: You have no idea what it is, but it has the words "Lytton P. D." inscribed upon it.
speaker: narrator

text:
Dredging up your ancient Egyptian,
you translate the hieroglyphs:
  "She of the golden hair."
speaker: narrator

text: This is an excerpt from the Rosella Stone!  There is a picture next to the message.
speaker: narrator

text: By golly, it's a rare Peruvian Onklunk!
speaker: narrator

text: You wonder if the portrait of Erasmus is really a painting, or something completely different.
speaker: narrator

text: Your eyes are drawn to the eyes of the figure in the portrait.  His intense stare is unnerving.
speaker: narrator

text: This would not be someone to trifle with, for he looks subtle and quick to anger.
speaker: narrator

text: This item appears to be an ebony chest. It could contain anything.
speaker: narrator

text: The table is bare, except for a stuffed bird which faces the door.
speaker: narrator

text: The books on the shelves of the bookcase appear to be very old, very rare, and very magical.
speaker: narrator

text: You find titles like:  "Zen and the Art of Magical Maintenance",  "How to Be a Halfling",  "The Sensuous Succubus",  and "Tobin's Spirit Guide, Volume II:  Asmodeus to Casper".
speaker: narrator

text: The shield looks like it belonged to one of those "Once and Future" kings.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a strange figure on a shelf above the stairs.  It looks like an overgrown rat wearing a wizard's hat.
speaker: narrator

text: An adumbrated wall hanging.  It's design evokes thoughts of ancient times and battles past.
speaker: narrator

text: There are two finely crafted chairs, one larger than the other.
speaker: narrator

text: Beneath the "Dunking Dragon" is an old steamer trunk.
speaker: narrator

text: The only actual door visible is the door leading back outside.
speaker: narrator

text: The staircase connects the upstairs to the downstairs.
speaker: narrator

text: The pattern is obscured by age.
speaker: narrator

text: The high ceiling has ornate sconcing and scrollwork.
speaker: narrator

text: Purple planking?
speaker: narrator

text: The wizard does not run an inn.
speaker: narrator

text: I wouldn't do that if I were you!
speaker: narrator

text: "Those rooms are forbidden!"
speaker: fenrus

text: "Since you're leaving anyway..."
speaker: fenrus

text: "That'll learn ya!"
speaker: fenrus

text: As the ghosts suck the life out of your body, and you crumple into the dust of the old graveyard, you think unspeakable thoughts of torture and torment directed at programmers, game designers, and vicious artists named Kenn.
speaker: narrator

text: The wall is too difficult for your level of skill, but keep practicing.
speaker: narrator

text: The large tombstone seems to have a barely legible epitaph:
speaker: narrator

text: "Here lies an atheist..."
speaker: narrator

text:
"All dressed up
and nowhere to go."
speaker: narrator

text: Growing up out of the grave is an evil-looking plant with a slimy, poisonous sheen.
speaker: narrator

text: The large stone you are facing has been engraved with this message:
speaker: narrator

text:
"Here lies Lester Moore
Gored six times by a wild boar."
speaker: narrator

text: "No Les, no Moore."
speaker: narrator

text: The carving on the gravestones, for the most part, seems to have been intentionally defaced and worn with time.
speaker: narrator

text: You sense what nobody needed to tell you:  SOMETHING is making those spirits fly around, and it isn't the wind.
speaker: narrator

text: You sense a spiritual coldness.
speaker: narrator

text: The only things that can be opened here are graves, and you don't want to open THOSE!
speaker: narrator

text: Your magical abilities are to no avail against the undead.
speaker: narrator

text: You can't see the bottom of the hole before you.
speaker: narrator

text: Nothing grows in the dirt of the old graveyard.  Some of the graves appear to have been disturbed.
speaker: narrator

text: You see transparent, decaying, writhing, slimy, undead fragments of undulating ectoplasm!
speaker: narrator

text: I DO believe in spooks!  I DO believe in spooks!  I DO, I DO, I DO believe in spooks!!
speaker: narrator

text: You recall from somewhere that the most likely time to find ghosts is at night.
speaker: narrator

text: The graves seemed to be placed haphazardly in the tiny graveyard.  All appear to be old and poorly maintained.
speaker: narrator

text: The town wall looks more massive from this side.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a very large and very dead tree which hovers over the graveyard.
speaker: narrator

text: The forest surrounds the graveyard on three sides.
speaker: narrator

text: You see the rotted remains of a picket fence.
speaker: narrator

text: What at first appears to be a ladder turns out to be the rotted remains of a picket fence.
speaker: narrator

text:
Let's see...root..root...
I know I put that root SOMEwhere!
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see any particularly interesting roots.
speaker: narrator

text: The red root growing out of one of the graves has a strange and evil appearance.
speaker: narrator

text: It appears that the spirits frolic here during the dark hours.
speaker: narrator

text: It's certainly creepy here even during the daylight hours.
speaker: narrator

text: Everything here is already dead.
speaker: narrator

text: It appears that you have company in the graveyard tonight.
speaker: narrator

text: This must be the town graveyard.  What a run-down place!
speaker: narrator

text: You stepped into a hole whose depth is unfathomable.  As you fall ever downward, you feel icy fingers clutching at you, and you think: "How could I be so careless?"
speaker: narrator

text: You wrench the mandrake plant free from the tombstone on which it has grown.  You hear a scream like that of a dying child as you yank the root from the ground.
speaker: narrator

text: You wrench the mandrake plant free from the tombstone on which it has grown.  Somehow you thought this would be a lot harder.  You wonder if you did this right.
speaker: narrator

text: The root shrivels and twists in your hands until nothing is left.  Astonished, you look at your empty hands.
speaker: narrator

text: C'mon now! That's HARDLY a heroic action!
speaker: narrator

text: GADZOOKS!
speaker: narrator

text: I'm sorry, but this game has a PG rating.
speaker: narrator

text: You didn't really say that, did you?
speaker: narrator

text: I'm not programmed to do that sort of thing!
speaker: narrator

text: That wouldn't be appropriate.
speaker: narrator

text: The pressure gets to you after a while, doesn't it?
speaker: narrator

text: Didn't your Momma teach you any better than that?
speaker: narrator

text: ODS BODKINS!
speaker: narrator

text: You see nothing special.
speaker: narrator

text: It is just as it appears.
speaker: narrator

text: It looks pretty normal.
speaker: narrator

text: It's not important.
speaker: narrator

text: "Yes, the weather does what the weather will do, does it not?"
speaker: townsperson

text: "Surely you jest!"
speaker: townsperson

text: "I'm not sure that I understand you"
speaker: townsperson

text: "You seem to be talking without saying very much."
speaker: townsperson

text: "Beg pardon?"
speaker: townsperson

text: "Welcome back! It is good to see you again. So many adventurers never return. Someday, you and I will go on an adventure together."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Hrrmph! Ahem! Yes, as I was saying....where were we?"
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Oh, what a glorious day it was! I...... Oh, hello there!"
speaker: wolfgang

text: "You fiendish witch! I will have you...... Oh, it's you, my friend!"
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Oh, Barnard, bravest of the brave, you cannot be gone from us so soon.... What? What? You're still here, my fellow adventurer?"
speaker: wolfgang

text:
We're sorry, but the pixel you clicked on is not in service at this time.  Please check your mouse and click again.
Thank you!
speaker: narrator

text: Of all the places to click on, you had to choose THIS one!
speaker: narrator

text: If there was something interesting and informative at this location on the screen, we would certainly have put up a much more interesting and informative message than this one.
speaker: narrator

text:
This is a pixel.
This is only a pixel.
If this had been a REAL picture element, you would have seen a REAL message.
speaker: narrator

text: You have reached the residence of Mr. and Mrs. Pixel.  We are unable to come to the screen right now, but if you leave your game and character at the sound of the beep...
speaker: narrator

text:
There is a place between shadow and substance...
between reality and imagination...
but THIS isn't it!
speaker: narrator

text: Yes!  You have found a cleverly hidden representation of the Starship You-Know Who!
speaker: narrator

text: You may have already won a fabulous, all-expense-paid trip for two to scenic Shapeir, land of the shifting sands.  To claim your prize, simply purchase a soon-to-be-released copy of "Quest for Glory II:  Trial by Fire" at a software dealer near you.
speaker: narrator

text: Congratulations!  By clicking at this spot on the screen, you have initiated an irreversible process that will destroy all life as we know it.  Have a nice day!
speaker: narrator

text: Day is dawning
speaker: narrator

text: It is mid-morning
speaker: narrator

text: It is midday
speaker: narrator

text: It is mid-afternoon
speaker: narrator

text: Sunset approaches
speaker: narrator

text: The night is still young
speaker: narrator

text: It is the middle of the night
speaker: narrator

text: It is not yet dawn
speaker: narrator

text: You see the purple home of someone who must enjoy its precarious perch upon the peak.
speaker: narrator

text: The stone creature looks like it's bored.  Wouldn't you be?
speaker: narrator

text: The cliff walls are steep, and it's a long way to the snow fields below.
speaker: narrator

text: You can see the mist flowing around the edges of the Wolf's Bane Peaks.
speaker: narrator

text: Below you, the snow glistens.
speaker: narrator

text: It is cloudless. It seems that you are above the clouds.
speaker: narrator

text: Looking down and to the south, you see the tortuous path you climbed to get here. You'd hate to have to do that again.
speaker: narrator

text: Looking below you to the west, you can see the Baron's Castle surrounded by the forest.
speaker: narrator

text: From outside, you can see that the interior of the house is curiously furnished.
speaker: narrator

text: It's about time the gargoyle opened the door for you.
speaker: narrator

text: You'd like to look at it more closely, if only the gargoyle would let you.
speaker: narrator

text: The door is already open.
speaker: narrator

text: You feel as though you have just scaled the Matterhorn in full armor.  What a climb!
speaker: narrator

text: After you finally catch your breath, you see that you have reached the rather eccentric-looking house that you saw from below.
speaker: narrator
